conflicts.description: The previous sheet placed Sylvia's discovery of Anya's knowingly false Desmond assessment at the chapter_17 review, but chapter_13 shows Anya admitting the distortion and Sylvia tracing its routing damage that same morning.
conflicts.canonical_value: Sylvia knows by chapter_13 that Anya knowingly falsified the Desmond assessment and that it changed WISE routing.
conflicts.resolution_note: Chapter_13 contains the explicit confession and Sylvia's contemporaneous damage-chain briefing. Chapter_17 is a reinstatement and warning after review, not the point of discovery.

conflicts.description: Chapter_04's ministry contacts sheet names Colonel Bretz as Emil, while chapter_05 dialogue has Margarethe call him Ludwig.
conflicts.canonical_value: Use Colonel Bretz without a canon given name.
conflicts.resolution_note: Both first names are single-instance mentions and neither is causally central. Title-only reference is the most stable choice, and the context pack also stays title-only.

conflicts.description: Chapter_03's document artifact timestamps Anya's false assessment at 08:14, while chapter_13's retrospective routing review labels it 09:14.
conflicts.canonical_value: Anya's false WISE assessment was filed at 08:14.
conflicts.resolution_note: The chapter_03 timestamp is a direct on-page document artifact, so it outranks the later retrospective mismatch in chapter_13.
